sections.description: Secure your WordPress login: passwordless passkeys (WebAuthn/FIDO2), security alerts by email or Slack/Discord webhook, trusted devices, custom login URL, login attempt logging, IP whitelist/blacklist with CIDR, brute-force protection, redirect rules, time-based access, two-factor authentication, and multisite support. Everything is verified on your own server in PHP with no external services. Modern appearance options including Dark Mode and Glassmorphism.
sections.changelog:
2.0.0

  • New - Passkeys (WebAuthn / FIDO2): passwordless, phishing-resistant sign-in using device biometrics, screen lock or a hardware security key. The whole ceremony is verified on your server in pure PHP (CBOR decoding, COSE key reconstruction and signature checks) with no external services. Passwordless and second-factor modes; supports ES256, RS256 and EdDSA credentials.
  • New - Security Alerts: email and/or Slack/Discord webhook notifications on lockouts, administrator sign-in from a new IP, and failed-login spikes, with per-type cooldowns and a test button.
  • New - Trusted Devices: users can skip the second factor on browsers they trust, via an HMAC-signed, server-side revocable cookie. Only the second step is skipped - the password or passkey is always required.

1.9.1

  • Security - Remediations from the bundled security audit: documented Application Password policy for two-factor users, authenticated (GCM) two-factor secret encryption, and related hardening.

1.9.0

  • New - Multisite support: a Network Admin settings screen for network-wide security policy (shared IP blacklist, force two-factor, force login limiting). New network sites are provisioned automatically.

1.8.0 – 1.8.1

  • New - Two-factor authentication (TOTP), compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy and 1Password. QR code generated entirely on your server, single-use recovery codes, plus administrator and wp-config recovery paths.

1.7.0

  • New - Time-based access restrictions (Schedule tab) with per-day and overnight login windows in your site timezone.

1.6.0

  • New - Custom login redirect rules by role or username, plus default login and logout redirects.

1.5.0

  • New - Progressive (escalating) lockouts with offence memory for persistent attackers.

1.4.0

  • New - IP blacklist and exclusive trusted-IP access mode, with CIDR (IPv4/IPv6) support.

1.3.0

  • New - Login attempt logging with a filterable Logs tab and automatic retention.
sections.installation: Upload the plugin to /wp-content/plugins/, activate, and configure under MBR → Login Customiser.
